seoTitle: How to Integrate ClickUp with Gmail
metaDescription: Learn how to install the ClickUp Gmail extension, turn emails into tasks, attach threads to existing cards, and reply without leaving ClickUp.
intro: Important requests often arrive by email but need to become trackable tasks somewhere else. This guide covers installing the ClickUp extension for Gmail, converting an open email into a new task with assignees and due dates, and attaching email threads to existing project cards so your communication history stays centralized.

steps.heading: Open the App Center
steps.body: In ClickUp, click your avatar and launch the App Center to find the Gmail integration.

steps.heading: Install from the Google Workspace Marketplace
steps.body: Search for Gmail, navigate to the Google Workspace Marketplace listing, and click Install.

steps.heading: Authorize your Gmail account
steps.body: Pass the Google OAuth consent screen and select the target Gmail account you want to connect.

steps.heading: Find the ClickUp icon in Gmail
steps.body: Open your inbox and locate the new ClickUp logo in the right-side action dock.

steps.heading: Create or attach a task from an email
steps.body: Click the extension icon inside an open email to create a new task or attach the message to an existing one.

steps.heading: Reply from inside ClickUp
steps.body: Use the native Email ClickApp on the task to review the attached content and send a reply without switching back to Gmail.

faq.question: Can I attach an email to a task that already exists?
faq.answer: Yes, the Gmail extension lets you attach an open email thread to an existing ClickUp task instead of only creating new ones.

faq.question: Do I need a Google Workspace account to use this?
faq.answer: You'll need a Gmail account to authorize the connection, and the extension is installed from the Google Workspace Marketplace.

faq.question: Can I reply to a client email without leaving ClickUp?
faq.answer: Yes, the native Email ClickApp lets you send a reply directly from the task's activity timeline.

faq.question: Does creating a task from an email carry over the sender's details?
faq.answer: You can set task parameters like status, priority, assignees, and target list directly from Gmail when converting an email into a task.
